content: The orthographic variant spelling 'reflexion' (an archaic alternate form of the modern 'reflection' widely taught in high school English curricula) can act as a natural 'perturbation seed' to trigger multi-perspective LLM self-reflection. Asking an LLM to first solve a problem using the 'reflexion' spelling framing, then compare it to solutions generated under the standard 'reflection' framing, elicits the inconsistent solving perspectives that current LLM self-reflection techniques rely on to improve output quality.
